thesis: Closed-loop adaptive interaction that infers perception, biomechanical intent, and workload to adjust multisensory feedback and interfaces.
problemStatement: Perceptual thresholds, force capacity, and cognitive load shift with motion (walking, driving, teleop) and user differences (VR familiarity, low vision). Fixed XR/vehicle/wearable systems can increase motion sickness, missed cues, unsafe glances, and assistance errors.
approachStatement: Sense (tactile/IMU/physio + context) → Infer (DT/JND, intent, workload) → Adapt (vestibular/foot, EMS/thermal/pressure, vibrotactile, UI/eHMI) → Optimize (glances, MS, DT, task, trust).
handoff1to2: DT/JND and divided attention (walking, driving, compression load) cap what actuation stays perceivable and comfortable.
handoff2to3: Stable, low-latency actuation enables task-level adaptation without destabilizing motion or increasing visual demand.

layers.name: SENSORY LAYER
layers.goal: Quantify and extend RDW and scanning perception limits (DT/JND, attention) with vestibular, foot, and nonvisual cues.
layers.keyImpact: Extends RDW DT (23–45%) and reorientation robustness while preserving presence/stability, and improves low-vision scanning without added discomfort.

layers.name: MOTOR LAYER
layers.goal: Infer force/intent from tactile/IMU data and deliver simulation-grounded EMS, thermal+pressure, and vibrotactile actuation.
layers.keyImpact: Enables calibrated EMS/thermal actuation and tactile-sensed control for VR teleop, walker stability (<1°), and screen-free CPR coaching (>92% models).

layers.name: BEHAVIOR LAYER
layers.goal: Adapt interfaces and feedback policies in driving, AV/XR passengers, teleop, training, and mobility under workload, timing, and safety constraints.
systemAdapted: PSA adaptive in-car touchscreens (profile + cognitive state)
In-car VR contextual cues + steering-gain limits
SYNC-VR + event-based posture tasks for MS reduction
layers.keyImpact: Adapts UI/eHMI/VR timing from workload and motion signals, mitigating distraction (≈20% slower selections under load) and motion sickness while improving trust/clarity.
